elements.title: Old South Meeting House
elements.contents: ... that British troops turned Boston's Old South Meeting House (pictured) into a horse-riding facility during the American Revolutionary War?

elements.title: Maula Prison
elements.contents: ... that Ethiopian migrants have to stay in Malawi's overcrowded Maula Prison until they can fund their own deportation?

elements.title: Fatahillah
elements.contents: ... that Fatahillah, billed as the saviour of the Indonesian film industry and at the time its most expensive domestic film, was a commercial failure?

elements.title: a Russian Air Force pilot
elements.contents: ... that a Russian Air Force pilot was convicted in absentia of war crimes after being exchanged in a prisoner swap?

elements.title: recover from the 1928 Okeechobee hurricane
elements.contents: ... that a mobster was kidnapped for three days while helping the United States Virgin Islands recover from the 1928 Okeechobee hurricane?

elements.title: National Sporting Club
elements.contents: ... that, when the National Sporting Club introduced the Lonsdale Belt in 1909, it guaranteed permanent holders a pension of £1 a week from age 50?

elements.title: award-winning poem
elements.contents: ... that the late Renée Good wrote an award-winning poem?

elements.title: Duos for Doris
elements.contents: ... that Duos for Doris was Keith Rowe and John Tilbury's first exclusive collaboration despite having worked with each other for around forty years?

elements.title: a Chinese activist
elements.contents: ... that a Chinese activist jet-skied 300 kilometres (190 mi) over 14 hours to escape to South Korea?
